title: Delicate Egyptian Lentil Curry
description: A fragrant, anti-inflammatory curry with panang paste, yuzu zest, and earthy lentils that captures the essence of a monsoon in Cairo.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a saucepan over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, grated ginger, and panang curry paste, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add rinsed lentils, coconut milk, and vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes, until lentils are tender.
Stir in yuzu zest, lime juice, and half the chopped cilantro, allowing flavors to meld for 2 minutes.
Taste and season with salt to preference, then remove from heat.
Transfer the curry to serving bowls and sprinkle remaining cilantro on top.
Serve hot with steamed jasmine rice or flatbread for a complete meal.
